skill.name: file-organizer
skill.description: Organizes local files by reviewing folder structure, finding duplicates, proposing safer cleanup plans, and moving files after user approval.
skill.summary: Helps review, organize, rename, archive, and deduplicate local files with approval-based cleanup plans.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: The static external-command findings are mostly true positives for shell examples, but they are documented local file-management commands rather than hidden executable code. The weak-cryptography finding is a low-risk use of md5 for duplicate detection, not authentication or encryption, and the reconnaissance matches show no malicious network or system reconnaissance intent. Publication is reasonable with a warning because the skill can inspect broad local directories and move or delete files after user approval.

medium_findings.title: Local File Operations Can Move or Delete User Data
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to create folders, move files, rename files, and recommend duplicate deletion. It also requires confirmation before deletion and asks for approval before execution, which reduces risk but does not remove the possibility of data loss from an unsafe plan or incorrect path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-management behavior is explicit and central to the skill. The built-in approval guidance lowers severity, so this is an operational safety risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Broad Local Filesystem Enumeration
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends commands that list directories, inspect file types, measure sizes, and enumerate files in a target directory. This is legitimate for organization work, but it can expose sensitive filenames, metadata, and folder structure to the AI session or tool logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell examples clearly enumerate local files and metadata. The purpose appears legitimate, with no evidence of exfiltration or covert collection.

low_findings.title: Weak Hash Used Only for Duplicate Detection
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged md5 as a weak cryptographic algorithm. In context, md5 is used to identify exact duplicate files, not to protect secrets, authenticate data, or encrypt content. Collision risk can still make it less reliable than stronger hashes for high-value cleanup decisions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The md5 command is visible in a duplicate-detection example. Its non-security use makes the high-severity static finding a false positive, but a low reliability concern remains.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands for Local File Inspection
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes shell commands such as ls, find, file, du, sort, uniq, and md5 for local filesystem analysis. These commands are expected for this skill but should be run only against user-approved directories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and legitimate for file organization. Risk depends on scope and user consent rather than hidden malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Commands That Modify Local Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes mkdir and mv examples for creating folders and moving files. These are legitimate cleanup operations, but incorrect paths or broad plans can disrupt user data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The modifying commands are directly shown and match the skill purpose. The document includes approval and logging rules, so this is elevated operational risk rather than malicious code.

content.user_title: Organize Files with Safer Cleanup Plans
content.value_statement: Messy folders make files hard to find and increase duplicate storage. This skill reviews local directories, proposes clear structures, and executes cleanup only after user approval.
actual_capabilities: Reviews a target directory and summarizes file types, sizes, dates, and structure issues.
Finds duplicate candidates by filename, size, or hash-based comparison.
Proposes folder structures for downloads, projects, photos, archives, and mixed document folders.
Creates cleanup plans that list folders to create, files to move, and decisions needing review.
Moves or renames files after approval and recommends maintenance habits.
limitations: Requires access to local filesystem tools and user-approved directories.
Cannot guarantee perfect categorization when file names or content are ambiguous.
Deletion recommendations still need human confirmation before execution.
Hash-based duplicate checks may not detect visually similar photos or near-duplicate documents.

use_cases.title: Clean a Downloads Folder
use_cases.description: Sort downloaded documents, installers, images, and temporary files into useful folders with a reviewable plan.
use_cases.target_user: Individual users with cluttered personal workspaces

use_cases.title: Prepare Project Archives
use_cases.description: Separate active projects from old work, create archive folders, and identify folders that need manual decisions.

use_cases.title: Reduce Duplicate Storage
use_cases.description: Find duplicate candidates, compare paths and dates, and choose which files to keep before deleting copies.
use_cases.target_user: Operations teams and power users managing shared folders

prompt_templates.title: Organize One Folder
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single messy folder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me organize this folder. First summarize what is inside, then propose a folder structure before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Find Duplicate Candidates
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate cleanup focused on wasted storage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find duplicate files in this directory. Group likely matches, explain which copy you recommend keeping, and ask before deleting anything.

prompt_templates.title: Restructure Project Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced organization for active and archived work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my project directories. Separate active work, archived projects, templates, and uncertain items into a clear plan for approval.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Cleanup Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a repeatable monthly file cleanup workflow for my workspace. Include review steps, naming rules, archive criteria, and safety checks.

output_examples.input: My Downloads folder has hundreds of mixed files. Help me clean it up.
output: A short summary of file types, folder count, large items, and old files.
A proposed structure for documents, media, installers, archives, and items needing review.
A confirmation request before folders are created or files are moved.

output_examples.input: Find duplicate documents and tell me which copies to keep.
output: A grouped duplicate list with file paths, sizes, and modification dates.
A recommendation for the safest copy to keep in each group.
A deletion checklist that requires explicit approval before action.

output_examples.input: Review my Projects folder and suggest a better structure.
output: A current-state summary that separates active, old, duplicate, and unclear projects.
A proposed structure for active work, archives, templates, and learning projects.
A list of moves, renames, and manual decisions to confirm.

best_practices: Start with one directory and review the proposed plan before allowing file moves.
Back up important folders before large reorganizations or duplicate cleanup.
Use archive folders for uncertain files instead of deleting them immediately.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad cleanup across an entire home directory without narrowing the scope first.
Do not delete duplicate candidates without checking paths, dates, and user intent.
Do not move active project files while related tools or builds are running.

faq.question: Can this skill delete files automatically?
faq.answer: It is designed to ask for confirmation before deletion. Users should approve each deletion group carefully.

faq.question: Does it read file contents?
faq.answer: It may inspect filenames, metadata, and file types. Content review depends on the user request and available tools.

faq.question: Can it organize photos by date?
faq.answer: Yes. It can propose year and month folders based on metadata or file modification dates.

faq.question: Is it safe for shared team folders?
faq.answer: Use it carefully. Start with analysis only, review the plan with owners, and back up important files.

faq.question: Can it find every duplicate file?
faq.answer: It can find many exact duplicate candidates. It may miss near-duplicates or files with changed content.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
